caption: Step 1: Open Zoho Invoice - free online invoicing tool for businesses
description: Open Zoho Invoice - free online invoicing tool for businesses
narration: And... action. Let's start by opening Zoho Invoice - free online invoicing tool for businesses. Every good walkthrough needs an establishing shot, and this is ours.

caption: Step 2: Open the free invoice generator page - no login required
description: Open the free invoice generator page - no login required
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We open the free invoice generator page - no login required. Now the viewer knows exactly where the story starts.

caption: Step 3: Enter business details: company name, contact, and address
description: Enter business details: company name, contact, and address
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We enter business details: company name, contact, and address. A little data entry here saves the same keystrokes on every future run.

caption: Step 4: Enter Bill To details for Acme Corp International
description: Enter Bill To details for Acme Corp International
narration: Quick scene change. We enter Bill To details for Acme Corp International. Nobody wins an award for manual retyping, so the automation can have this one.

caption: Step 5: Set invoice number to INV-2026-001
narration: Cue the montage. We set invoice number to INV-2026-001. Locking this down keeps the workflow sharp on every replay.

caption: Step 6: Add line item 1 with quantity, rate, and GST
description: Add line item 1 with quantity, rate, and GST
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We add line item 1 with quantity, rate, and GST. It is a nice reminder that the workflow can grow without turning brittle.

caption: Step 7: Add line item 2: UI/UX Design services
description: Add line item 2: UI/UX Design services
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We add line item 2: UI/UX Design services. One more layer, and the story gets richer without getting messier.

caption: Step 8: Add line item 3: annual server hosting
description: Add line item 3: annual server hosting
narration: Quick scene change. We add line item 3: annual server hosting. This extra beat gives the demo a little more range without breaking its rhythm.

caption: Step 9: Add payment notes and terms for the client
description: Add payment notes and terms for the client
narration: Cue the montage. We add payment notes and terms for the client. It is a nice reminder that the workflow can grow without turning brittle.

caption: Step 10: Review subtotal, GST breakdown, and grand total
description: Review subtotal, GST breakdown, and grand total
narration: Now the camera swings to the next beat. We review subtotal, GST breakdown, and grand total. Now the plot thickens, in the best spreadsheet kind of way.

caption: Step 11: Use Download/Print to export the invoice as PDF
description: Use Download/Print to export the invoice as PDF
narration: Here comes the payoff shot. We use Download/Print to export the invoice as PDF. This is where the workflow earns its applause.

caption: Step 12: Tutorial complete - create professional GST invoices in minutes
description: Tutorial complete - create professional GST invoices in minutes
narration: Roll credits. The flow is captured end to end and ready for a replay. The flow is now captured end to end and ready to replay or adapt.
